title: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

Graham Platner's downfall threatens Democrats' chances of winning US Senate
subhead: The Democratic candidate for the November Senate race in Maine, a representative of the populist left-wing faction shaking up the Democratic Party, was forced to withdraw on Wednesday after a series of scandals.

title: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
subhead: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

title: Kylian Mbappé: A leader on a mission, both on the pitch and behind the scenes at the 2026 World Cup
subhead: The captain of the French team, which faces Morocco on Thursday in the quarterfinals, keeps scoring goals while playing an active role behind the scenes. His selflessness stands in stark contrast to the criticisms of his supposed individualism.

title: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
subhead: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.

title: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
subhead: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.
